summary: Assistant Professor Matthew Jones is working to decode molecular processes on the genetic, epigenetic, and microenvironment levels to anticipate how and when tumors evolve to resist treatment.
author: Lillian Eden | Department of Biology

title: How Joseph Paradiso’s sensing innovations bridge the arts, medicine, and ecology
summary: From early motion-sensing platforms to environmental monitoring, the professor and head of the Program in Media Arts and Sciences has turned decades of cross-disciplinary research into real-world impact.
author: David Sweeney | Media Lab

title: Improving AI models’ ability to explain their predictions
summary: A new approach could help users know whether to trust a model’s predictions in safety-critical applications like health care and autonomous driving.
author: Adam Zewe | MIT News

title: A “ChatGPT for spreadsheets” helps solve difficult engineering challenges faster
summary: The approach could help engineers tackle extremely complex design problems, from power grid optimization to vehicle design.
author: Adam Zewe | MIT News

title: Featured video: Coding for underwater robotics
summary: Lincoln Laboratory intern Ivy Mahncke developed and tested algorithms to help human divers and robots navigate underwater.
author: Erin Lee | Tim Briggs | MIT Lincoln Laboratory

title: New method could increase LLM training efficiency
summary: By leveraging idle computing time, researchers can double the speed of model training while preserving accuracy.
author: Adam Zewe | MIT News

title: AI to help researchers see the bigger picture in cell biology
summary: By providing holistic information on a cell, an AI-driven method could help scientists better understand disease mechanisms and plan experiments.
author: Adam Zewe | MIT News

title: Exposing biases, moods, personalities, and abstract concepts hidden in large language models
summary: A new method developed at MIT could root out vulnerabilities and improve LLM safety and performance.
author: Jennifer Chu | MIT News

title: Parking-aware navigation system could prevent frustration and emissions
summary: By minimizing the need to drive around looking for a parking spot, this technique can save drivers up to 35 minutes — and give them a realistic estimate of total travel time.
author: Adam Zewe | MIT News

title: Personalization features can make LLMs more agreeable
summary: The context of long-term conversations can cause an LLM to begin mirroring the user’s viewpoints, possibly reducing accuracy or creating a virtual echo-chamber.
author: Adam Zewe | MIT News
